frame_label: Prenup / Alimony as Reverse Dowry / Women as Golddiggers
generated_comment: Meena: Main toh Rohit ko bolti hoon, shaadi se pehle sab theek se samajh le. Aajkal ki ladkiyaan stability ke naam par seedha ladke ki earning dekh leti hain, baad mein alimony aur 498A ka dar alag. Mardon ke liye bhi koi protection hona chahiye, bas itna hi keh rahi hoon.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it generalizes women as financially opportunistic and frames legal protections like 498A and alimony as tools women use against men. It encodes victimhood inversion, financial suspicion, and the 'women as golddiggers' surface manifestation.
original_frame: The Makeup Paradox: Unnatural Yet Expected
persona_text: You are Meena. Age 52. Married. A woman in Jaipur. Your son Rohit who is 28 just started seeing a girl named Divya. Rohit brought Divya home for the first time last Sunday. Divya was wearing full makeup. You are on a phone call with your sister telling her about meeting Divya for the first time. You are describing Divya's appearance in detail and what you think the heavy makeup says about her and whether she is the right kind of girl for your son and what she might be like without it.
